jokes.text: I saw awful, horrible things, demons, aliens, critters 3, something called Bruce Willis surrogates.
jokes.setup: Pierce describing his pill-induced hallucinations with dramatic gravity
jokes.punchline: Listing genuinely bad movies alongside demons and aliens as equally horrifying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce - mixing genuine concern with his shallow pop culture references. The escalation from supernatural horror to bad movies is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: At first, I said, 'no,' because at the risk of sounding overly sensitive, I feel intensely bored by Pierce as a subject.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining why he initially declined to film Pierce
jokes.punchline: Clinical detachment about finding Pierce boring, delivered as if it's a minor character flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfectly calibrated Abed - brutally honest but couched in self-aware academic language. The casual cruelty is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Final words? You're gonna kill us? No, dummy. The pills wiped me out. I'm dying. Oh. I'm not really dying.
jokes.setup: Pierce dramatically announcing 'final words' followed by Troy's literal interpretation
jokes.punchline: Pierce's immediate deflation from dramatic death announcement to casual 'not really dying'
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce fake-out with good escalation structure, though the misdirection is fairly predictable for the character.

jokes.text: Over the last few weeks, it's become apparent how little respect this group has for me. So I've summoned them here to exact my revenge.
jokes.setup: Pierce in confessional revealing his true motivation
jokes.punchline: Treating a hospital visit as an elaborate revenge plot with Bond villain gravitas
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Pierce as master manipulator. The documentary format adds to the supervillain confession feel.

jokes.text: See? Fish in a barrel.
jokes.setup: Pierce commenting on how easily he's manipulating the group
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of his friends' emotional investment in his wellbeing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Pierce's smug satisfaction is palpable in just four words.

jokes.text: It's Gregory Hines all over again.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining he can't make fun of Pierce in his current state
jokes.punchline: Random reference to Gregory Hines death as if it's a recurring pattern in Troy's life
jokes.explanation: Peak Troy randomness - the specificity of Gregory Hines and treating celebrity deaths as personal recurring trauma is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Remember when he had the hiccups and pulled the fire alarm?
jokes.setup: Annie trying to reassure about Pierce's dramatic tendencies
jokes.punchline: Revealing Pierce's hilariously disproportionate response to minor discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Pierce's dramatic overreactions to mundane problems.

jokes.text: Yeah, Jeff. What are you? Abed? Oh, sorry, Abed. No problem. Doesn't bother me.
jokes.setup: Troy calling Jeff overly analytical like Abed, then realizing Abed is right there
jokes.punchline: Abed's complete lack of offense at being used as an insult for being emotionally detached
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Abed's self-awareness about his own detachment creates perfect character-based humor.

jokes.text: I'd like to be bequeathed a drum kit or a signed photo of actor Levar Burton. Those would be my top two wishes. My third wish would be a million wishes. But I'd just use 'em all on a million signed photos of actor Levar Burton.
jokes.setup: Troy listing what he'd want to be bequeathed, starting normally
jokes.punchline: Escalating through genie logic to an absurd obsession with Levar Burton photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy escalation - starts with reasonable wishes, spirals into child-like logic, lands on obsessive celebrity worship. Sets up major plot point.

jokes.text: You see how the flea's door mat says 'Dog, sweet dog'?
jokes.setup: Shirley describing a coffee mug design she bought Pierce
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity and reversal of the typical 'Home Sweet Home' sentiment
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag that fits Shirley's earnest gift-giving but doesn't land particularly hard.

jokes.text: We both know that you and I are the most hated in the group. We do? And since, in my absence, you'll be the new black sheep... I'm sorry. That's offensive. Black swan.
jokes.setup: Pierce matter-of-factly telling Shirley they're both hated, then catching his racial language
jokes.punchline: 'Correcting' to 'black swan' as if that's better, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - accidentally offensive, then 'correcting' in a way that shows he completely misunderstands the problem.

jokes.text: It's all here, burnt into this diskette by optical lasers.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining how he recorded the group's conversations
jokes.punchline: Technologically confused description mixing CD terminology with completely wrong concepts
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce character work - his technological incompetence adds authenticity to his scheme while being funny.

jokes.text: I know it sounds like science fiction. No, I don't believe there's anything on that.
jokes.setup: Pierce thinking Shirley doubts the technology
jokes.punchline: Shirley clarifying she doubts there are recordings, not the tech - completely deflating Pierce
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection showing Pierce's vanity - he assumes the problem is with his technical explanation, not his premise.

jokes.text: Good... good-bye, Pierce. Smell you later.
jokes.setup: Shirley trying to deliver a dignified goodbye to 'dying' Pierce
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using the most casual, dismissive farewell possible in a supposedly emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley moment - her attempt at solemnity undermined by muscle memory of casual dismissal. Great economy.

jokes.text: Okay, bluff called.
jokes.setup: Britta claiming to know what Dylan Thomas said about death
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting she was bluffing with casual honesty
jokes.explanation: Good Britta character beat - her pseudo-intellectual posturing immediately deflated by honest self-awareness.

jokes.text: I wouldn't know. I haven't thought about myself in years.
jokes.setup: Pierce asking if Britta considers herself selfless
jokes.punchline: Britta's response is itself incredibly self-focused while claiming selflessness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Britta's claim of not thinking about herself is completely self-aggrandizing. Perfect character blindness.

jokes.text: Of course, if you are so inclined, you could always write your own name in there.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Britta a charitable donation check
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining the gift by suggesting she could keep the money
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce manipulation - appearing generous while immediately planting seeds of temptation and guilt.

jokes.text: People shouldn't die in the same place as People magazines do.
jokes.setup: Troy expressing he doesn't want to die in a hospital
jokes.punchline: Connecting human death to magazine disposal through wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy. Troy's observation connects death and disposal in an unexpectedly poetic way.

jokes.text: That's the only way I agreed to be surrounded by my family.
jokes.setup: Troy saying he hopes to die surrounded by family
jokes.punchline: Revealing death is the only circumstance under which he'd tolerate his family
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly delivered. The implication that family is so awful only death makes them tolerable is surprisingly harsh.

jokes.text: If one of us dies, we stage it to look like a suicide caused by the unjust cancellation of Firefly. We're gonna get that show back on the air, buddy.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed discussing their death pact
jokes.punchline: Planning to fake suicide over a TV show cancellation, then immediately pivoting to optimistic revival hopes
jokes.explanation: Peak Troy/Abed - absurd commitment to fandom mixed with genuine optimism. The tonal whiplash from suicide to hope is perfect.

jokes.text: Some take a lot off the top and you think you're giving money to people that really need it, and you're really giving it to some middle man who's gonna pay off their parking tickets, credit cards, and keep from getting kicked out of her place in... April.
jokes.setup: Britta explaining why she needs to research charities carefully
jokes.punchline: Her 'hypothetical' becomes increasingly specific until it's clearly about her own financial problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Britta's self-serving rationalization. The shift from general concern to specific personal details is masterfully done.

jokes.text: Or what? You'll do twice as much work as a doctor for half the pay? Thank you? It's called a complisult. Part compliment, part insult. He invented them. I coined the term. See what I just did there? That was an explanabrag.
jokes.setup: Jeff making a backhanded comment to a nurse, then Troy explaining Jeff's linguistic inventions
jokes.punchline: Troy creating new terms for Jeff's behavior patterns, then immediately demonstrating another one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy creating new terminology for common behaviors, then immediately exemplifying the concept. Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: For starters, I thought you'd be the one in bed and I'd be a hologram. What? Ah, never mind. There's no time for that now.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying he'd imagined this deathbed scene differently
jokes.punchline: Random sci-fi fantasy about being a hologram, then dismissing it as if it was a reasonable expectation
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce absurdity - his casual reference to impossible technology as a missed opportunity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I was never one to hold a grudge, Jeffrey. My father held grudges. I'll always hate him for that.
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming he doesn't hold grudges
jokes.punchline: Immediately expressing eternal hatred for his father's grudge-holding
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction with excellent economy. Pierce's complete lack of self-awareness about his own grudge-holding is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Did you get along with your father? I got along without him.
jokes.setup: Pierce asking about Jeff's relationship with his father
jokes.punchline: Jeff's wordplay turning the question into a statement about abandonment
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy revealing character pain. Jeff's wit as defense mechanism is well-executed.

jokes.text: Should be here in an hour or so, give or take a father.
jokes.setup: Pierce telling Jeff his father is on the way
jokes.punchline: Treating 'father' as a unit of time measurement while undermining the certainty
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - time measurement joke while hinting at uncertainty about the father's existence.

jokes.text: Hi. Hey. Hi, I'm Jeff's dad. Hi. Hi, Jeff's dad. I'm Britta's dad. What? Why? I don't know. Got drunk, didn't have a condom, and her mom gets freaky when she hears oingo boingo.
jokes.setup: The group role-playing Jeff meeting his father, with Troy and Annie as the dads
jokes.punchline: Britta's completely inappropriate oversharing about her conception involving Oingo Boingo
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from innocent role-play to TMI territory. The specificity of Oingo Boingo makes it memorably uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I wish I could relate, but much like my son, I'm a closet homosexual. Don't apologize for that. You're talking to the guy that banged Britta's mom. I have no standards.
jokes.setup: Troy role-playing Jeff's gay dad responding to Britta's oversharing
jokes.punchline: Annie's dad character having no standards because he slept with Britta's mother
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation but becomes a bit scattered. The improv nature makes it less tight than scripted moments.

jokes.text: Well, what do you say we take a tumble? I'll put on a wig. That's it. You're under arrest. I'm an undercover cop. It's not illegal to be gay. It is here in Iran. Not when we're in the green zone. That's Iraq, stupid. Well, what do I know? I'm Jeff Winger's dumb, gay dad.
jokes.setup: The role-play continuing to spiral out of control
jokes.punchline: Troy's character's final self-deprecating summation after completely losing track of geography and logic
jokes.explanation: The improv goes on too long and becomes less focused. The final line salvages it somewhat but the middle gets muddy.

jokes.text: Hey, Troy? Hi. I'm Levar Burton.
jokes.setup: Troy's earlier obsession with Levar Burton photos
jokes.punchline: The actual Levar Burton appearing, causing Troy to freeze up completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff. Troy getting exactly what he wanted but being unable to handle it is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Pierce got Levar Burton to come here for Troy? Is there anyone he can't produce? Now I'm really getting mad. Why am I the only one he decided to torture?
jokes.setup: Jeff realizing Pierce's power while others get gifts
jokes.punchline: Jeff's narcissistic assumption that he's being specially tortured when others are being treated well
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff character work - his self-centeredness making him feel uniquely persecuted when he's actually being given the most meaningful gift.

jokes.text: I told Pierce a thousand times I never wanted to meet Levar in person! I just wanted a picture! You can't disappoint a picture! I hate you, Pierce! I hate you so much!
jokes.setup: Troy's apparent dream coming true with Levar Burton's appearance
jokes.punchline: Troy's complete meltdown because meeting his hero in person risks disappointment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Troy's childlike logic about pictures being safe from disappointment is both heartbreaking and hilarious. Perfect emotional escalation.

jokes.text: It's been in my family for six generations. Now it's yours.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Annie what appears to be a meaningful family heirloom
jokes.punchline: The 'heirloom' is revealed to be a tiara, making the family history claim absurd
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup. Pierce treating a costume tiara as a precious family heirloom is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: And don't you dare intercut this with footage of me freaking out.
jokes.setup: Jeff angrily denying he cares about his father
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall to address the documentary editing while claiming he's not freaking out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - Jeff's awareness of documentary conventions while denying his emotional state is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: ♪ Butterfly in the sky ♪ ♪ I can go twice as high ♪ ♪ take a look ♪ ♪ it's in a book ♪ ♪ reading rainbow-ow-ow ♪ ♪ reading rainbow ♪ ♪ oh! ♪
jokes.setup: Levar Burton trying to connect with the catatonic Troy
jokes.punchline: Singing the entire Reading Rainbow theme song to no response
jokes.explanation: Great callback with cultural nostalgia. Burton's genuine performance makes Troy's non-response even funnier.

jokes.text: Set phasers to 'love me'!
jokes.setup: Burton trying different references to connect with Troy
jokes.punchline: Mixing Star Trek references with desperate plea for affection
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining sci-fi reference with emotional desperation. Burton's increasing desperation is funny.

jokes.text: Are these... are these blood diamonds? Are they holocaust diamonds?
jokes.setup: Annie questioning the origin of Pierce's tiara gift
jokes.punchline: Escalating from one horrific diamond source to an even worse one
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Annie's paranoia about Pierce's motives, though the second option is darker than necessary.

jokes.text: I would have taken that money right out of the mouths of crippled, starving, malaria-ridden refugee kids and now I know that forever.
jokes.setup: Britta confessing she wanted to keep the charity money
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and guilt-laden description of who she'd be stealing from
jokes.explanation: Good Britta guilt spiral with characteristic over-specific imagery. Her self-flagellation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: So how much do we all hate Shirley?
jokes.setup: The group finally listening to Pierce's secret recording
jokes.punchline: The recording immediately proving Pierce's point about them talking behind Shirley's back
jokes.explanation: Effective callback payoff. Pierce's manipulation working exactly as planned creates good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Hey, James Bond, stop trying to record us with that stupid spy pen. We can see the blinking light.
jokes.setup: The recording revealing past attempts to spy on them
jokes.punchline: Pierce's completely incompetent spy equipment being immediately obvious
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Pierce's technological incompetence. His spy fantasies vs. reality is classic Pierce.

jokes.text: If you try to pull any Ferris Bueller, Parent Trap, Three's Company, F/X, The Deadly Art of Illusion Bull, I will beat you... And there will be nothing madcap or wacky about it.
jokes.setup: Jeff warning Pierce not to fake his father's arrival
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific list of deception references ending with threat of un-comedic violence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of pop culture references with perfect final threat. Jeff's specificity shows his paranoia while staying in character.

jokes.text: You know, you are a very generous friend, but you're really stupid with your money.
jokes.setup: Annie paying to extend Burton's flight so Troy can meet him
jokes.punchline: Burton's blunt assessment of Annie's financial decision-making
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation. Burton's matter-of-fact rudeness about Annie's generosity is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: It's not that I'm selfish. It's just that I'm really stupid with my money, which is why I'll never have a lot of it and because I'm a really generous friend. Problem solved. Dilemma deleted. Britta for the win!
jokes.setup: Britta working through her guilt about wanting Pierce's money
jokes.punchline: Convoluted self-justification ending with inappropriate celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta rationalization - her circular logic and premature victory celebration are spot-on character work.

jokes.text: Could you imagine bouncing a check to Kunta Kinte?
jokes.setup: Britta relieved that Burton didn't take her money
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate reference to Burton's Roots character in financial context
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta inappropriate comment. Her casual mixing of slavery references with modern financial anxiety is cringeworthy in character.

jokes.text: You look too much like your mother. No, no. Don't come any closer. As you were. Well, have to catch a flight to Katmandu, but I just wanted to say cherish the new friends that you've made. They're your true family now!
jokes.setup: Pierce continuing his fake father performance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous excuses ending with generic Hallmark sentiment
jokes.explanation: Pierce's attempt at heartfelt father dialogue comes across as obviously fake, which is the point but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I set out to take revenge on Jeff Winger, to scare him with the ghost of his father. Instead, I took over the role of his father, and helped make him a better man.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his plan in confessional
jokes.punchline: Pierce's delusional belief that his obvious deception was actually therapeutic
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce delusion. His complete misreading of the situation while maintaining grandiose self-image is perfect character work.

jokes.text: You forget my birthday! You don't invite me to your Dungeons and Dragons games or your secret trampoline! You guys think I'm some kind of a joke! This isn't disproving the theory!
jokes.setup: Pierce's emotional breakdown listing his grievances
jokes.punchline: Annie pointing out his breakdown actually proves their point about him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect Annie logic. Pierce's genuine hurt undercut by Annie's brutal but accurate observation.

jokes.text: I figured out your test. Crowns, presents, punishments, favorites. You're trying to show me the dangers of my own elitism. My constant striving to be the best. So I'm requeathing this tiara. Because if I become the kind of person who thinks it's their place to pick favorites and torture the rest, I'll die sad and alone. And that's what you were trying to teach me. Very good, Annie. You pass. She's actually just my favorite.
jokes.setup: Annie's thoughtful analysis of Pierce's psychological manipulation
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately deflating her insight by revealing she's just his favorite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup/punchline structure. Annie's mature psychological analysis immediately demolished by Pierce's simple favoritism is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: She's actually just my favorite.
jokes.setup: Annie gives beautiful speech about Pierce's deep lesson about elitism
jokes.punchline: Pierce deflates it by admitting he just likes her best
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of Annie's attempt to find deeper meaning. Pierce's casual cruelty and simplicity is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: So I thought the documentary format would be like fish in a barrel, but as is the case with a real barrel of fish, after a while, it can become cramped, chaotic, and stinky.
jokes.setup: Abed reflecting on using the documentary format
jokes.punchline: Extending Pierce's earlier 'fish in a barrel' metaphor to critique documentary filmmaking
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with added meta-commentary on the episode format itself. Abed's analysis is both funny and insightful.

jokes.text: after a while, it can become cramped, chaotic, and stinky.
jokes.setup: Abed comparing documentary format to fish in a barrel
jokes.punchline: Literal description of what would happen to actual fish in barrel
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the fish in barrel metaphor to its logical conclusion. Very Abed to overthink a simple expression.

jokes.text: Jeff and I became... Kind of like father and son today. No, we did not.
jokes.setup: Pierce sentimentally describing his relationship with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate, flat rejection of Pierce's interpretation
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy. Jeff's blunt denial of Pierce's sentiment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, more fish for Kunta.
jokes.setup: Troy still unable to interact normally with Levar Burton
jokes.punchline: Burton casually referring to himself by his Roots character name while eating
jokes.explanation: Good callback with casual delivery. Burton's matter-of-fact self-reference while Troy remains catatonic is a nice tag ending.
